qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of Galaxy and for whom is it intended?
qa_pairs.answer: Galaxy is an open source, web-based platform for data-intensive biomedical research. Its primary purpose is to make computational biology accessible to research scientists who do not have computer programming or systems administration experience.

qa_pairs.question: How do Canadian users access the UseGalaxy Canada platform and what initial storage allocation do they receive?
qa_pairs.answer: Canadian users can access UseGalaxy Canada by navigating to [https://starthere.usegalaxy.ca](https://starthere.usegalaxy.ca), logging in through CILogon, and selecting their institution for authentication. The platform automatically allocates Canadian users a default starting storage of 500 GB.

qa_pairs.question: What are some key technical characteristics of the UseGalaxy Canada platform, such as its servers and management?
qa_pairs.answer: The UseGalaxy Canada platform operates on Beluga Cloud and Arbutus servers. All Galaxy and server configurations, as well as management and updates, are handled by the UseGalaxy Canada team. Users are not required to have Linux knowledge, and pre-installed tools are synchronized with UseGalaxy.eu.
